name: Claude: Photo library of all products for presentations
Mekyle appraisal
notes:
Asana Workflows that affect you.

1. **Job Satisfaction:**
- How satisfied are you with your current role and responsibilities?
- Are there aspects of your job that you find particularly fulfilling or frustrating?

2. **Career Development:**
- Do you feel you have opportunities for growth and development within the company?
- What skills or areas would you like to develop further?

3. **Team Dynamics:**
- How do you feel about the team’s collaboration and communication?
- Are there any team dynamics that you think could be improved?

4. **Work Environment:**
- Do you feel the work environment supports your productivity and well-being?
- Are there any changes you would suggest to improve the workplace atmosphere?

5. **Management Support:**
- Do you feel supported by your manager and the leadership team?
- Are there specific ways management can better support you in your role?

6. **Company Culture:**
- How do you perceive the company culture, and what do you value most about it?
- Are there any cultural aspects you think we should emphasize more or change?

7. **Feedback and Recognition:**
- Do you feel your contributions are recognized and appreciated?
- How do you prefer to receive feedback and recognition?

8. **Work-Life Balance:**
- How do you find your current work-life balance?
- Are there any adjustments that could help you maintain a healthier balance?

9. **Goals and Expectations:**
- Are your personal and professional goals aligned with the company's objectives?
- Do you feel clear about what is expected of you in your role?

10. **Suggestions for Improvement:**
- What suggestions do you have for improving the company’s policies or processes?
- Are there any resources or tools you need to perform your job better?

notes:
Technician for smart home company.

We are looking for a technician to install the following systems for projects in the Western Cape:
    CCTV
    Audiovisuals
    Networks
    Smart lighting
    Access control

The candidates need to have experience installing and configuring these systems. As well as cabling for them and building racks.

Other useful experience includes IT installations and working on building sites. 

The candidate needs to have a valid ID, drivers license, a clear police record.

At least 2 years of experience installing the above systems.

Candidates with a passion for technology need to have good communication skills, be able to work effectively independently, and as a team. 

They need to have good time management skills. Be presentable and act as ambassadors for the company. Trustworthiness, customer service, and pride in their work are a must, as these align with our company values. 

Send CVs to admin@d-one.co.za

Currently, we have 3 people logging time against the budgets in harvest projects. So we are effectively logging 3 hours of time on site against 1 hour of quoted time budgeted. 

Please see if there is a way to have installation time logged so that each technician accounts for half of the budgeted hour. Since we bill for a team of 2 technicians. 

This might be in the billable amount allocated to each technician (should be R700/2 not R700). Or if it’s strictly time against time we might be stuck.

name: Weekly winning report
notes:
Submit this report weekly by the end of Friday. 

This will be used to track your KPIs and scorecard.

If you attain a suitable score for this by the end of the year, you will move from installer and team leader, to project manager next year. 

Each of these subtasks represents 1 point.

So you will get a score out of 5 per week. There are 100 points up for grabs.

For this role, your score needs to be above 80 by the end of the year. It starts 1 August. There are some bonus points available in July if you want to get ahead/practice.

There will be 4 bonus points up for grabs for meeting each of the values. 
1. Flow - things move smoothly and naturally.
2. Art - installations and comms are complete, neat and professional.
3. Service - the answer is always yes, how can I help.
4. Trust - you can rely on me, I've got this. 

Please comment on ways that we can empower, measure and grow the rest of the team.

name: AUTOMATE D-ONE Victory Conditions
notes:
What you hope to get out of our coaching engagement is the following:
a. A plan with milestones and priorities.
b. Someone to talk through issues and thinking and keep you to the vision.
c. Clarity of the vision and to stop chasing shiny pennies
d. Define what is winning is and to stop the monotony.
e. Be clear on your personal Victory Conditions in order find the motivation.
2. Based on the first session there are 3 key themes and tactics in the following order:
a. Free up Darren.
b. Sales and marketing 1st. Get some new clients through new and existing channels.
c. Hire the right people and holding all staff accountable [This requires clarity on delivery +
some new meeting rhythms]

4. Your Victory conditions over the three periods are as follows [at this stage]:
a. In 3 years:
i. This is your “Dream state:”
ii. 10 staff with high levels of accountability and trust
iii. The business operates but you are not responsible for the delivery
iv. A “machine” with components feeding the machine

name: My Roles
notes:
Time track your own work and break it into hours per task, high/low impact, high/low
complexity and where it would sit in the business verticals [HR, Sales, Finance etc]. This
will be used to determine who to hire next, delegate and outsource in order to free up
your time.

name: Strategy Matrix
notes: https://miro.com/app/board/uXjVO6gY42A=/

name: Customer Avatars
notes:
Once you understand what it its you are selling (primary product) and what makes this product or service unique (USP) and ultimately what the client would gain from buying from you (benefits) you can decide who would be the perfect client to approach to buy from your company.
    The Customer/Client Avatar is what your perfect client would like today and for the next period. This should be revisited quarterly or when large projects are on boarded as well as
when new employees are hired within the business.
3. Some
a. What size business? E.g. corporate, family owned, multinational or parastatal. (This
of the questions that help outline the avatar are as follows:
can also be determined by their revenue or profits)

name: Product
notes: The primary product is the product or services that you as a business will be speaking the most about.

name: USP
notes:
The
i. You can’t be seen as another “Me Too” business.
    We know what it is your business offers but in order to get new meetings, get more work out of existing clients and retain clients you need to position the offering in a unique way.
    You need to find something you believe in and then use this as another reason to convince clients to buy from you and buy from you NOW.
    Start with a long format word document that outlines what makes what you do unique and different?
    What Unique Problem are you Solving for your customer? (UPS)
    List 3-5 of the biggest benefits a client gets from choosing to work with you that they
    could not get from someone else

name: Sales strategy
notes:
Selling your product or service without planning how you will go about it is likely to yield far poorer results than if you adopt a strategic approach to your sales.
    Use a sales strategy as part of your business plan to help you set out in detail how you will get your product or service in front of people who need it. Looking at it strategically will give you a comprehensive, methodical approach to ensuring you marketing your business correctly and you are approaching the right clients.
    A sales strategy can be based on your business and marketing plans.

name: 8 layer strategy framework
notes:
In unpacking the most suitable strategy methods for my clients, I make sure to have
them thoroughly understand this approach and how to apply it to their thinking in order
to achieve some kind of business objective in the next 12 months.
b. This strategic approach is split into 8 layers that provide insight and analysis into the
goals and measurements required to get some solid work done within a particular
window period.
c. Let’s take a strong, hard look at how exactly it works and in what ways it will help your
business shine.
i. The Situation, The Victory Conditions and The Goals
1. When looking at this multi-layered approach, there are first a list of
questions that need to be asked
